title: Fuel Oil Trader
description:
Requisition ID: R000068414 Fuel Oil Trader
Job Location: Singapore – Duo Tower

To submit application, please click below posting link or copy and paste to a new browser:  
https://chevron.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com/jobs/job/Singapore-DUO-Tower-Singapore/Fuel-Oil-Trader_R000068414

Responsibilities for this position may include but are not limited to:

  • Trades in both physical and financial commodities in the Asian and international trading markets, both spot and term, in pursuit of both trading for profit and system optimization.
  • Origination of buy and sell side clientele focusing on building flow, optimization and trading around it.
  • Develops trading strategies, initiates and manages physical and financial positions for profit, focuses on asset optimization, and manages price exposure of trading positions.
  • Develop arbitrage flows as part of the Global Fuel Oil Commodity teams.
  • Utilizes the company's position and assets to leverage market opportunities and increase enterprise earnings.
  • Understands trading tools and methodology in order to collaborate with analysts to model and evaluate market conditions and supply and trading scenarios.
  • Ensure that all activities are within appropriate delegations of authority and conform with Risk Manual Guidelines while demonstrating strong operational excellence.

Required Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree
  • 5+ years of trading experience in fuel oil with strong background in both physical and financial trading.  
  • Knowledge of multiple commodities in different regions, or globally, with experience in working arbitrage flows.
  • Ability to develop trading strategies for short to long term time horizons inter-regionally for physical and financial commodities in pursuit of system optimization and trading for profit.
  • Ability to understand market alternatives and utilize that knowledge to make decisions in the best interest of the enterprise.
  • Strong leadership, teamwork, communication, and analytical skills.
  • Knowledge of the role of supply and trading on a large corporate scale.
  • Strong entrepreneurial and negotiation skills.
  • Understands commodity risk and reward concepts and evaluation techniques.
  • Understands the importance of measuring, managing, and reporting basis risk and financial exposures.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree
  • Experience in trading fuel oil and associated infrastructure, customer, and fundamental market knowledge
  • Experience in building flow, optimization and trading around it.

postedCompany.description:
Singapore is the regional headquarters for chevron's downstream operations in the asia-pacific region.


Chevron has a significant presence in Singapore, dating back to 1933. The company employs around 1,100 people in the country and are involved in several key areas:


Regional Headquarters. Singapore is the regional headquarters for Chevron’s downstream operations in Asia-Pacific including manufacturing, marketing, and lubricants businesses.


Caltex Brand Management. The Caltex brand, which serves customers in Asia and the Middle East, is managed from Singapore.


Refining. Chevron has a 50 percent interest in the Singapore Refining Company (SRC).


The SRC refinery processes up to 290,000 barrels of crude oil per day, producing liquefied petroleum gas, gasoline, jet fuel, diesel, fuel oil and asphalt. Recent upgrades have enabled the refinery to produce higher-value gasoline that meets stricter emission standards.


Lubricant Additives. Chevron Oronite’s regional headquarters for Asia Pacific and home to one of the largest fuel and lubricant additives manufacturing facilities in Asia is in Singapore.


Shipping and Storage. Chevron has a key presence in Singapore’s vital shipping industry with a blending plant and a terminal with over 400,000 cubic meters of storage capacity.


Trading. Chevron’s Supply & Trading organization provides commercial support to our crude oil and natural gas production operations and to our refining and marketing network and has a regional trading hub in Singapore that partners with our headquarters in Houston, Texas and other trading hubs in London, UK and San Ramon, California.


Chevron New Energies (CNE). Is the arm of Chevron focused on developing lower carbon solutions in the Asia Pacific region.  CNE established its headquarters in Singapore 2021 and aims to help customers meet their lower carbon goals which includes carbon capture utilization and storage, hydrogen, and renewable fuels.


In Singapore, Chevron hires full-time employees with relevant education and experience in the following areas:


• Asset Commercialization

• Business Development

• Category Management

• Procurement

• Finance

• Information Technology/Data Analytics

• Shipping

• Supply & Trading


What We Offer:


• Comprehensive and competitive remuneration that are made up of short and long-term components.

• Hybrid, Flexible work-life balance options

• Ongoing learning and career development
postedCompany.name: CHEVRON SINGAPORE PTE. LTD.
